heading: Carbon storage validation and testing
label: 16293 Carbon storage validation and testing

Wage Rate RequirementsFor provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of , including authority of Secretary of Labor, see .Pub. L. 117–58section 18851 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: demonstrates the ability to inject into geologic formations and sequester carbon dioxide; and

children.label: B
children.text: has a goal of sequestering not less than 50 million metric tons of carbon dioxide.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 2 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program of research, development, demonstration, and commercialization for carbon storage.

children.label: A
children.text: in coordination with relevant Federal agencies, developing and maintaining mapping tools and resources that assess the capacity of geologic storage formation in the United States;

children.label: i
children.text: to predict carbon dioxide containment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to account for sequestered carbon dioxide in geologic storage sites;

children.label: i
children.text: potential environmental, safety, and health impacts in the event of a leak into the atmosphere or to an aquifer; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any corresponding mitigation actions or responses to limit harmful consequences of such a leak;

children.label: D
children.text: evaluating the interactions of carbon dioxide with formation solids and fluids, including the propensity of injections to induce seismic activity;

children.label: E
children.text: assessing and ensuring the safety of operations relating to geologic sequestration of carbon dioxide;

children.label: F
children.text: determining the fate of carbon dioxide concurrent with and following injection into geologic formations;

children.label: G
children.text: supporting cost and business model assessments to examine the economic viability of technologies and systems developed under the program;

children.label: H
children.text: providing information to the Environmental Protection Agency, States, local governments, Tribal governments, and other appropriate entities, to ensure the protection of human health and the environment; and

children.label: I
children.text: evaluating the quantity, location, and timing of geologic carbon storage deployment that may be needed, and developing strategies and resources to enable the deployment.

children.label: A
children.text: operating oil and gas fields;

children.label: B
children.text: depleted oil and gas fields;

children.label: C
children.text: residual oil zones;

children.label: D
children.text: unconventional reservoirs and rock types;

children.label: E
children.text: unmineable coal seams;

children.label: F
children.text: saline formations in both sedimentary and basaltic geologies;

children.label: G
children.text: geologic systems that may be used as engineered reservoirs to extract economical quantities of brine from geothermal resources of low permeability or porosity; and

children.label: H
children.text: geologic systems containing in situ carbon dioxide mineralization formations.

children.heading: Large-scale carbon sequestration demonstration program
children.label: c Large-scale carbon sequestration demonstration program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a demonstration program under which the Secretary shall provide funding for demonstration projects to collect and validate information on the cost and feasibility of commercial deployment of large-scale carbon sequestration technologies.

children.heading: Existing regional carbon sequestration partnerships
children.label: 2 Existing regional carbon sequestration partnerships
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Secretary may provide additional funding to regional carbon sequestration partnerships that are carrying out or have completed a large-scale carbon sequestration demonstration project under this section (as in effect on the day before ) for additional work on that project.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Demonstration components
children.label: 3 Demonstration components
children.text: Each demonstration project carried out under this subsection shall include longitudinal tests involving carbon dioxide injection and monitoring, mitigation, and verification operations.

children.label: A
children.text: existing or completed demonstration projects receiving additional funding under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: any new demonstration projects funded under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: assesses the progress of all regional carbon sequestration partnerships carrying out a demonstration project under this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: identifies the remaining challenges in achieving large-scale carbon sequestration that is reliable and safe for the environment and public health; and

children.label: C
children.text: creates a roadmap for carbon storage research and development activities of the Department through 2025, with the goal of reducing economic and policy barriers to commercial carbon sequestration.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may transition large-scale carbon sequestration demonstration projects under subsection (c) into integrated commercial storage complexes.

children.label: A
children.text: to identify geologic storage sites that are able to accept large volumes of carbon dioxide acceptable for commercial contracts;

children.label: B
children.text: to understand the technical and commercial viability of carbon dioxide geologic storage sites; and

children.label: C
children.text: to carry out any other activities necessary to transition the large-scale carbon sequestration demonstration projects under subsection (c) into integrated commercial storage complexes.

children.heading: Large-scale carbon storage commercialization program
children.label: e Large-scale carbon storage commercialization program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a commercialization program under which the Secretary shall provide funding for the development of new or expanded commercial large-scale carbon sequestration projects and associated carbon dioxide transport infrastructure, including funding for the feasibility, site characterization, permitting, and construction stages of project development.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be eligible to enter into an agreement with the Secretary for funding under paragraph (1), an entity shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: is open to projects at any stage of development described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: facilitates expeditious development of projects described in that paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: projects with substantial carbon dioxide storage capacity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: projects that will store carbon dioxide from multiple carbon capture facilities.

children.heading: Preference in project selection from meritorious proposals
children.label: f Preference in project selection from meritorious proposals

children.text: with respect to the research, development, demonstration program components described in subsections (b) through (d) give preference to proposals from partnerships among industrial, academic, and government entities; and

require recipients to provide assurances that all laborers and mechanics employed by contractors and subcontractors in the construction, repair, or alteration of new or existing facilities performed in order to carry out a demonstration or commercial application activity authorized under this section shall be paid wages at rates not less than those prevailing on similar construction in the locality, as determined by the Secretary of Labor in accordance with subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40, and the Secretary of Labor shall, with respect to the labor standards in this paragraph, have the authority and functions set forth in Reorganization Plan Numbered 14 of 1950 (15 Fed. Reg. 3176; 5 U.S.C. Appendix) and .section 3145 of title 40

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: g Cost sharing
children.text: Activities carried out under this section shall be subject to the cost-sharing requirements of .section 16352 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $2,500,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
